## Learning Objectives

- Develop basic R programming skills (e.g., creating vectors, performing operations, installing and using packages) 
 ￼
- Apply fundamental statistical functions such as mean, median, minimum, maximum, and standard deviation  ￼

## Notes

We will use the following notes to cover material for Lab 1

1. [Fundamentals of R](https://ucla-stats13.netlify.app/notes/01-fundamentals)
2. [Stats 10 Lab 1 Handout](https://app.box.com/s/af91nv5gy97atmdt6uwsghnzce6znfmd)

<!-- 2. [Importing Data in R](https://ucla-stats13.netlify.app/notes/02-importing_data)
3. [Summarizing Numerical Data](https://ucla-stats13.netlify.app/notes/03-intro_numerical_variables)
4. [Summarizing Categorical Data](https://ucla-stats13.netlify.app/notes/04-intro_categorical_variables) -->


## Questions

1.  Create a code chunk below this question and print the statement:
    "`My name is (your name) and my UID is (your UID). I am looking forward to Stats 10!`"

2.  Create a vector named `titles` and fill it with at least 3 book
    titles. Print the vector `titles`.

3.  Run the code chunk below using `titles`. Read the error message and
    explain why an error occurs.



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
sum(titles)
```
:::



4.  Create a vector named vector_log and make it hold the values `TRUE`,
    `FALSE, TRUE, FALSE`. Try multiplying the values by 2 and using the
    `mean()` function. Explain your results and how R treats logical
    values.


5.  You are given a vector containing the scores of 50 students on a
    recent exam. Find the minimum, maximum, mean, and standard deviation
    of the scores. Print each value (for example, print "Mean: \_\_\_").



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# You do not have to change this code
set.seed(123456789)
scores <- sample(0:100, 50, replace = TRUE)
print(scores)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
 [1]  65  15  43  44  85  38  11  31   8  43   9  25  20  22  37  57  14  43  69
[20]  67  12  45   8  31  65 100  19  92   6  44  76  51  48  71   0  72  78  66
[39]  79  44  14  82  12  84  48  42  22  45  45  38
```


:::
:::




6.  Using the `scores` vector, find the mean value without using the
    `mean()` function. (Hint: The mean is calculated by finding the sum of all values and dividing by the total number of values.)


-   Explain whether there is a difference between the means found in #5
    and #6 and why it does or does not exist.

7.  Knit the file into a PDF and name it "*yourname_yourUID Stats 10 Lab
    1*". Submit the file into Canvas.
